name: Helmod
author: Helfima
summary: In-game factory planner with matrix solver. Highest downloads of any planner; preferred by users for complex byproduct loops and oil chains.
notes: Most-downloaded planning mod (~521K). Updated for 2.0/Space Age. Reputation: powerful but steeper learning curve than Factory Planner; users prefer it for complex situations and 'input-dependent' planning.

name: Even Distribution
author: 321freddy

popular_threads.title: Include 'Even Distribution' and 'Rate Calculator' mods in base game
What mods are you using - and why?

summary: Ctrl+drag spreads stacks evenly across machines/chests; Shift+C cleans inventory. Perennially in 'must-have QoL' lists.
notes: Frequently mentioned in r/factorio 'best QoL mods' threads. ~502K downloads. Forum threads have proposed bundling it into base game alongside Rate Calculator.

name: Rate Calculator
author: raiguard

popular_threads.title: Does anyone know a good rate calculator?
Include Even Distribution and Rate Calculator in base game

summary: Drag-select tool that reports max production/consumption rate for the chosen machines. No upfront recipe modeling required.
notes: Maintained by raiguard, a Wube employee. Universally recommended in 2.0/Space Age threads as the easiest way to validate ratios in-place. ~400K downloads.

name: Factory Planner
author: Therenas

popular_threads.title: Questions about Helmod or Factory Planner

summary: Cleaner UI alternative to Helmod for in-game production planning. Dynamically adjusts UI when Space Age is enabled.
notes: Created explicitly because Helmod was 'difficult to learn'. Active 2.0 maintenance (latest release 20 days before research). Known limitation: does not handle fluids of different temperatures correctly.

name: Recipe Book
author: raiguard
summary: Compendium of recipes/materials/machines with advanced filtering. Modern successor to FNEI; pre-computes data on save load.
notes: Generally seen as the modern replacement for FNEI; some SeaBlock users prefer FNEI's search ergonomics. Recipe Book pre-calculates data at load (slower load, faster runtime).

name: Bottleneck Lite
author: raiguard

popular_threads.title: What mods are you using - and why?

summary: Adds working/idle indicator dots to assemblers and drills. Zero runtime overhead via working_visualisations.
notes: Considered the de-facto successor to the original Bottleneck mod for 2.0; community recommends it specifically because it scales without performance impact on large factories.

name: factoriobin.com

popular_threads.title: Introducing FactorioBin - the fastest, easiest way to share your blueprint strings

summary: Pastebin-style site purpose-built for blueprint strings. Renders a basic preview from the embedded icons; no search, no accounts.
notes: Recommended in r/factorio specifically as a Pastebin replacement (Pastebin retroactively deletes blueprints). Author keeps scope deliberately small. No 2.0/Space Age compatibility note on site, but blueprint strings still work.

name: factorio.school

popular_threads.title: Top blueprint library suggestion on the Factorio subreddit

summary: Successor to Factorio Prints with tagged search, books, and curated collections. Cited as 'the most feature-rich' sharing site.
notes: Reddit discussions consistently call it 'School wins on features'. Active hosting of Space Age content (DocJade's AutoMall, Nilaus books).

name: factorioprints.com

popular_threads.title: Top blueprint library suggestion on the Factorio subreddit

summary: Original community blueprint repository. Largely superseded by factorio.school (same author lineage); referenced as the historical archive.
notes: Multiple comparisons describe it as 'basically discontinued' or 'old-work archive vibes' versus factorio.school. Still up and accessible; community defaults to school for new uploads.

name: Factorio Codex (web)

popular_threads.title: Space Age Mall + Early Game Military Mall

summary: Newer blueprint database with built-in factory calculator and structure-view rendering rather than just strings. Aimed at the 2.0 era.
notes: Comparisons describe it as 'the new search experience of the 2.0 era'. Smaller library than school/prints but actively curated for Space Age. Note: in-game mod by the same name exists, separate project.

name: BlueprintBot (Discord)

popular_threads.title: Made a Discord bot for Factorio Servers

summary: Discord bot that renders blueprint strings to images via /blueprint and /book commands. Backed by the FBSR Java renderer.
notes: Standard tool in Factorio Discord servers. Powered by Factorio-FBSR (open source); the same engine drives reddit u/BlueprintBot mentions historically.

name: Production Calculator (Google Sheets, by Lupoviridae)

popular_threads.title: Production Calculator Google Sheet [2.0 Space Age & Vanilla]

summary: Google Sheet that produces ratios, machine counts, belt/inserter counts, and oil-cracking balances. Has explicit 2.0 and Space Age sheets.
notes: One of the few spreadsheet calculators actively maintained for 2.0. Quality and new beacon mechanics still pending in the sheet at last update.

name: Factorio Cheat Sheet
summary: Single-page web reference: smelting columns, oil ratios, beacon math, train signal cheat sheet. Frequently linked when newcomers ask 'what ratios should I use?'.
notes: Not a calculator per se; a curated cheat sheet. Treated as canonical link in r/factorio answers about ratios.

name: Even Pickier Dollies
author: hgschmie
summary: Nudge placed entities one tile at a time without losing wires/settings. Fork of Picker Dollies maintained for 2.0 after the original lapsed.
notes: Original Picker Dollies stalled on 2.0 update; community migrated to Even Pickier Dollies, a from-scratch rewrite. Useful while building blueprints by hand.
